I compile Modules/FortranCInterface/main.F using lfortran installed via pkg on a FreeBSD system, it results in an error. Is this an issue with lfortran or with CMake?

**System Info**

FreeBSD Version: 14.1-RELEASE

lfortran Version:  0.36.1

cmake Version: 3.30.5

**Error Output**

```shell
warning: #ifdef ignored
 --> main.F:3:1
  |
3 | #ifdef CALL_MOD
  | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ help: use the '--cpp' command line option to preprocess it

warning: #endif ignored
 --> main.F:5:1
  |
5 | #endif
  | ^^^^^^^ help: use the '--cpp' command line option to preprocess it

semantic error: Function 'call_sub' not found or not implemented yet (if it is intrinsic)
 --> main.F:2:11
  |
2 |           call call_sub()
  |           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```

Is this an issue with `lfortran` or with `CMake`?
